title: erodium aethiopicum subsp. pilosum
intro: Erodium aethiopicum subsp. pilosum, a species of flowering plant in the Geranium family, is commonly known as the hairy Stork's Bill or Pilose Stork's Bill. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to have various medicinal benefits. The health benefits of Erodium aethiopicum subsp. pilosum include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a diuretic and anti-diarrheal agent. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing its flowers or roots in water or oil, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
